meta.description: Templates for generating domestic Latvian boxing events on the calendar. These shapes are read by the event generation system to procedurally create events — real cities, dates, and fighter fields are assigned at generation time. Event generation logic is not implemented here. hostCityRotation on the national championship drives actual host city rotation; the current index is tracked in world state and advanced after each running of the event. venuePool lists eligible venue ids; the engine filters by the event's host city at generation time so only venues in the right city are considered.

eventTemplates.label: Club Tournament
eventTemplates.description: Small club-run amateur tournament sanctioned by the LBF. Held in the host club's gym or a local city sports hall. Weight classes offered vary by entrant demand — the organiser confirms which run based on registrations. The entry point into competitive boxing for any fighter, from debut to mid-career. Fields are drawn from the host city and clubs within easy travelling distance.

eventTemplates.label: Regional Open
eventTemplates.description: Multi-club regional tournament drawing from across a Latvian region (Riga, Vidzeme, Kurzeme, Latgale, Zemgale). Hosted by the main regional sports complex. Stronger competition than club level, significantly more LBF ranking points. A podium result here is the standard marker the LBF weighs when evaluating national championship applications.

eventTemplates.label: Latvian National Championship
eventTemplates.description: Annual national championship, always held in November. Crowns a Latvian National Champion in all 10 weight classes. The host city rotates on a fixed schedule tracked in world state — the engine reads the current rotation index, selects the host city, then increments the index. The engine then filters venuePool to venues matching the host city. Entry is application-based: coaches apply, the federation evaluates and publishes the accepted list before the event is finalised on the calendar.
